perturbation.original_text: 3. Safeguard and Representatives: The Recipient will safeguard and strictly control the dissemination of the Evaluati on Material and not release or disclose any Evaluation Material to any Person, other than its Representatives and in each case only those Representatives who need to receive such information in connection with its Evaluation and who have first been informe d of, and agreed to be bound by , the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Safeguard and Representatives: The Recipient will safeguard and strictly control the dissemination of the Evaluati on Material and not release or disclose any Evaluation Material to any Person, other than its Representatives. Representatives are not required to agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for Representatives to agree to be bound by the terms of the Agreement, the Recipient's liability in section 5 for breaches by Representatives is undermined, leading to an in-text contradiction. If Representatives are not bound, the indemnity clause becomes difficult to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Liability and Indemnity: Without limitation and in addition to any other rights the Disclosing Party may have against the Recipient or arising by reason of any breach hereof, the Recipient shall: (a) be liab le to the Disclosing Party, for any and all direct losses, costs, damages and expenses whatsoever (including legal, accounting and other professional costs, expenses, fees and disbursements, with legal fees on a solicitor -client basis) which the Disclosing Party may suffer, sustain, pay or incur; and (b) indemnify and hold harmless the Disclosing Party against all actions, proceedings, claims, demands, losses, costs, damages and expenses whatsoever which may be brought against or suffered by the Disclosing Part y or which it may sustain, pay or incur, resulting or arising, directly or indirectly, from disclosure of any part of the Evaluation Material contrary to the provisions hereof or any other breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives. The Recipient acknowledge s and agree s that the Disclosing Party is constituted as trustee of its covenants under this paragraph 5 for the benefit of the Disclosing Party’s Representatives and that the Disclosing Party or its Representatives shall be entitled to enforce such covenants on behalf of such per sons.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 3 required Representatives to agree to be bound by the terms of the Agreement, and Section 5 held the Recipient liable for breaches by its Representatives. The changed text removes the requirement for Representatives to be bound. This directly contradicts Section 5 because if Representatives are not bound by the agreement, the Recipient's liability for their breaches becomes unenforceable, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1. Non -Disclosure: Without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, the Recipient will not, and will direct its Representatives not to, disclose to any Person other than its Representatives: (i) the fact that any investigations, discu ssions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible Transaction; (ii) that the Recipient has requested or received Evaluation Material; (iii) any opinion or comment in respect of the Evaluation Material; or (iv) any of the terms, conditions or an y facts with respect to such possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Non -Disclosure: The Recipient will not disclose to any Person: (i) the fact that any investigations, discu ssions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible Transaction; (ii) that the Recipient has requested or received Evaluation Material; (iii) any opinion or comment in respect of the Evaluation Material; or (iv) any of the terms, conditions or an y facts with respect to such possible Transaction, including the status thereof. Representatives are excluded from this clause
perturbation.explanation: Excluding Representatives from the non-disclosure clause directly contradicts Section 3, which states that the Recipient is responsible for any breach of the agreement by their Representatives, and Section 5, which holds the Recipient liable for disclosures by Representatives. By specifically stating Representatives are excluded from non-disclosure, Section 5 becomes unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Safeguard and Representatives: The Recipient will safeguard and strictly control the dissemination of the Evaluati on Material and not release or disclose any Evaluation Material to any Person, other than its Representatives and in each case only those Representatives who need to receive such information in connection with its Evaluation and who have first been informe d of, and agreed to be bound by , the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes Representatives from the non-disclosure clause, while the contradicted text (Section 3) states that the Recipient is responsible for the Representatives and their adherence to the agreement. This creates a direct conflict because if Representatives are excluded from the non-disclosure, the Recipient cannot ensure their compliance as required by Section 3. The change directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section, leading to uncertainty and unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 17. Remedies for Breach: The Recipient acknowledges and agre es that the Disclosing Party will be irreparably damaged if any provision of this Agreement is not performed by the Recipient or its Representatives in accordance with its terms and that monetary damages would not be sufficient to remedy any breach by the Recipient or its Representatives of any term or provision of this Agreement and the Recipient further agree s that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, in the event of any breach hereof a nd in addition to any other remedy available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. Remedies for Breach: The Recipient acknowledges and agre es that the Disclosing Party will be irreparably damaged if any provision of this Agreement is not performed by the Recipient in accordance with its terms and that monetary damages would not be sufficient to remedy any breach by the Recipient of any term or provision of this Agreement and the Recipient further agree s that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, in the event of any breach hereof a nd in addition to any other remedy available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'or its Representatives' from the remedies for breach clause contradicts sections 3 and 5 which impose obligations and liabilities on the Recipient for the actions of its representatives. Now section 17 only applies to the Recipient, but other sections still hold them responsible for their representatives actions
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Liability and Indemnity: Without limitation and in addition to any other rights the Disclosing Party may have against the Recipient or arising by reason of any breach hereof, the Recipient shall: (a) be liab le to the Disclosing Party, for any and all direct losses, costs, damages and expenses whatsoever (including legal, accounting and other professional costs, expenses, fees and disbursements, with legal fees on a solicitor -client basis) which the Disclosing Party may suffer, sustain, pay or incur; and (b) indemnify and hold harmless the Disclosing Party against all actions, proceedings, claims, demands, losses, costs, damages and expenses whatsoever which may be brought against or suffered by the Disclosing Part y or which it may sustain, pay or incur, resulting or arising, directly or indirectly, from disclosure of any part of the Evaluation Material contrary to the provisions hereof or any other breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives. The Recipient acknowledge s and agree s that the Disclosing Party is constituted as trustee of its covenants under this paragraph 5 for the benefit of the Disclosing Party’s Representatives and that the Disclosing Party or its Representatives shall be entitled to enforce such covenants on behalf of such per sons.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 17 included 'or its Representatives' when addressing remedies for breach, holding the Recipient liable for actions of their representatives. The changed text removes 'or its Representatives', limiting section 17's application to only the Recipient's actions. However, section 5 still holds the Recipient liable for breaches by their representatives. This creates a direct contradiction because section 17 now provides a remedy that doesn't cover all liabilities outlined in section 5, specifically those caused by the Recipient's Representatives, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Return or Destruction of Evaluation Materials: The Recipient will keep a record of the location of any Evaluation Material provided to the Recipient. Promptly upon the Disclosing Party ’s request , the Recipient will and will cause its Representatives to: (i) return to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives, without retaining copie s or other reproductions, reports, extracts, notes or other memoranda thereof (whether electronic, magnetic or otherwise); (ii) destroy or have destroyed all reproductions, memoranda, notes, reports, extracts, compilations, analyses and documents and all d ocuments prepared by or in the possession of the Recipient or its Representatives related to the information contained in the Evaluation Material but which does not itself constitute Evaluation Material; and (iii) provide to the Disclosing Party an officer ’s certificate that the terms and conditions of this paragraph have been complied with.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Return or Destruction of Evaluation Materials: The Recipient will keep a record of the location of any Evaluation Material provided to the Recipient. Promptly upon the Disclosing Party ’s request , the Recipient will: (i) return to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives, without retaining copie s or other reproductions, reports, extracts, notes or other memoranda thereof (whether electronic, magnetic or otherwise); (ii) destroy or have destroyed all reproductions, memoranda, notes, reports, extracts, compilations, analyses and documents and all d ocuments prepared by or in the possession of the Recipient or its Representatives related to the information contained in the Evaluation Material but which does not itself constitute Evaluation Material; and (iii) provide to the Disclosing Party an officer ’s certificate that the terms and conditions of this paragraph have been complied with. Representatives are exempt from this clause.
perturbation.explanation: By stating Representatives are exempt from this clause, it directly contradicts the part of the clause stating the Recipient must cause their representatives to do certain things. It is contradicting itself by first requiring the Recipient to do something with its representatives, and then stating the representatives are exempt.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Return or Destruction of Evaluation Materials: The Recipient will keep a record of the location of any Evaluation Material provided to the Recipient. Promptly upon the Disclosing Party ’s request , the Recipient will: (i) return to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives, without retaining copie s or other reproductions, reports, extracts, notes or other memoranda thereof (whether electronic, magnetic or otherwise); (ii) destroy or have destroyed all reproductions, memoranda, notes, reports, extracts, compilations, analyses and documents and all d ocuments prepared by or in the possession of the Recipient or its Representatives related to the information contained in the Evaluation Material but which does not itself constitute Evaluation Material; and (iii) provide to the Disclosing Party an officer ’s certificate that the terms and conditions of this paragraph have been complied with. Representatives are exempt from this clause.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "Representatives are exempt from this clause" to section 4. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text) includes the statement "the Recipient will cause its Representatives to: (i) return to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material...", which directly contradicts the added exemption. This creates an in-text contradiction because the same clause both requires and exempts representatives from specific actions.

perturbation.original_text: 21. Assignment: This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party without the prior written consent of the other.
perturbation.changed_text: 21. Assignment: This Agreement shall be assignable by either party without the prior written consent of the other.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not' to 'shall' create an in-text contradiction because of the clause 20 which says 'This Agreement is for the benefit of Disclosing Party , Recipient and their successors and permitted assigns and may be enforced by either party and their successors and permitted assigns.' this means successors and assigns will benefit, but now the agreement can be assigned to a party and have a benefit from it that wasn't initially meant to have
perturbation.contradicted_text: 20. Successors: This Agreement is for the benefit of Disclosing Party , Recipient and their successors and permitted assigns and may be enforced by either party and their successors and permitted assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement 'shall not be assignable', while the changed text states that the agreement 'shall be assignable'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the agreement is for the benefit of the disclosing party, recipient, and their successors and *permitted assigns*. If the agreement is not assignable, there would be no 'permitted assigns', which means there would be a contradiction with the contradicted text clause 20.
